config.goal: Reduce Brent fold1 h7-h8 max MAPE to 5% or lower using AAForecast-based methodology on the fixed 8-week forecast setup.

notes: Brent only. Fixed 8-week oil forecast remains unchanged. h1~h4 similarity is acceptable.
Mechanical success rule updated by operator: h7 and h8 MAPE up to 5% are acceptable, implemented as max(h7_mape,h8_mape) <= 5.
All variables in df.csv may be used; feature selection is unconstrained; Optuna and detailed hyperparameter edits are allowed.
Use AAForecast methodology as the primary line of attack; if details are unclear, consult ask-gemini with reference/aaforecast.pdf or the existing summary artifact.
original_goal: $codex-autoresearch Brent only. h7 and h8 MAPE up to 5% is acceptable. Use AAForecast methodology, all df.csv variables allowed, Optuna and detailed settings allowed, fixed 8-week forecast stays fixed.
prompt_text:
Goal: On Brent only, drive the fixed 8-week forecast so that fold_idx=1 horizon steps 7 and 8 each reach acceptable error, operationalized as max(h7_mape,h8_mape) <= 5. The metric is mechanical and must be computed only by `.omx/research/research-20260401-brent-aaf-h6h8/verify_brent_h7_h8_max_mape.py`. Lower is better; stop only when metric <= 5.

Current baseline under the current retained AAForecast recipe is about 17.8176 on the new metric.

Hard constraints:
- Brent only.
- Future oil forecast horizon stays fixed at 8 weeks.
- h1~h4 being merely similar is acceptable.
- Do not switch away from AAForecast methodology as the base method; refinements should remain AAForecast-style.
- When uncertain about the paper method, consult ask-gemini and `reference/aaforecast.pdf` (existing artifact: `.omx/artifacts/gemini-aa-forecast-paper-summary-20260401T130931Z.md`).
- All variables in `data/df.csv` may be used. Feature selection is unrestricted. Optuna tuning is allowed. Detailed model/training settings may be modified freely.
- Never stage or revert unrelated dirty files already in the repo. Prefer new or isolated artifacts under `.omx/research/research-20260401-brent-aaf-h6h8/**`.

Recommended search directions:
1. Directly target h7/h8 late-spike behavior, not h6.
2. Expand Brent exogenous inputs aggressively from df.csv, including broader shock/risk proxies.
3. Add AAForecast-specific controls (season_length, trend_kernel_size, anomaly_threshold, attention width, encoder depth/width, dropout) and tune them with Optuna if useful.
4. If needed, extend the AAForecast implementation while preserving the package-authoritative/public-model boundary and non-stage-plugin rule.
5. Keep experiments causal and mechanically verified.

Artifacts to maintain:
- `.omx/research/research-20260401-brent-aaf-h6h8/autoresearch_eval/summary_h7_h8.json`
- `.omx/research/research-20260401-brent-aaf-h6h8/autoresearch_eval/brent_h7_h8_points.json`
- any additional experiment notes or helper files under the same folder.

Guard command must keep passing. If the run finds a materially better retained keep, log it before starting the next experiment.
